doom.driver: Iran says it targeted American base after fresh US strikes. The hostilities come during a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran, and protracted negotiations to end the three-month war.
doom.blurb: CEASEFIRE IN NAME ONLY, MISSILES IN PRACTICE
doom.summary: The US and Iran are conducting mutual strikes during active peace negotiations, which is either the world's most violent diplomacy or the world's most diplomatic violence — hard to tell. Kuwait activated air defenses, oil jumped 2%, and Trump reportedly threatened to bomb Oman, a US ally, which is the kind of threat that suggests the 'negotiating on fumes' line applies to everyone in the room. With a fragile ceasefire absorbing direct hits from both sides, the definition of 'ceasefire' is doing a lot of heavy lifting right now.

factors.name: Geopolitics
factors.detail: US and Iran exchanging strikes during active peace talks; Kuwait air defenses activated; Strait of Hormuz shipping threat resurfaces with oil prices jumping 2%.

factors.name: Governance
factors.detail: Trump threatening ally Oman amid Iran escalation signals erratic coalition management; ballot seizures in four states raise midterm integrity concerns.

factors.name: AI Capability
factors.detail: Anthropic and OpenAI reportedly reaching product-market fit while AI agents are now authorized to trade stocks on Robinhood — autonomy expanding faster than oversight.

factors.name: Alignment
factors.detail: Tech CEOs described as suffering 'AI psychosis' per TechCrunch; graduates booing AI at commencement suggests the hype-reality gap is becoming publicly legible.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: Bangladesh measles outbreak kills 500+, Ebola spreading in active conflict zones with DRC ceasefire called for specifically to enable response — disease-conflict convergence accelerating.

doom.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, no text, no antialiasing — extreme close-up filling the entire frame with a crumpled Persian-language newspaper lying on a dark ceramic kitchen tile floor, camera pressed directly overhead, the paper's front page dominated by a massive block-printed headline in Farsi script rendered in deep crimson ink, the newsprint yellowed and slightly damp at one corner, a small glass of black tea in a tulip-shaped Persian estakan sitting just at the upper edge of frame casting a warm amber pool of light across the creased paper, the tea's surface rippling faintly, steam rendered as two or three pale pixel wisps dissolving into the surrounding dark charcoal shadow, the newspaper's folds creating deep ink-black crevasses across the page, a partial photograph visible through the crease showing the blurred silhouette of a military structure against an orange sky, the tile grout lines framing the scene in a dark grid, a second folded page just visible beneath the first suggesting more news underneath, color palette locked to deep crimson and charcoal with the single warm amber accent of the tea glass, high contrast between the lit newsprint and the surrounding shadow, the overall mood heavy and suffocating, firelight-quality warmth bleeding only through the tea.

dawn.driver: Scientists break 30-year superconductivity record at normal pressure. Scientists at the University of Houston have shattered a long-standing superconductivity record, creating a material that can conduct electricity with zero resistance at the highest temperature ever achieved at normal pressure.
dawn.blurb: ZERO RESISTANCE: PHYSICS HISTORY REWRITTEN TODAY
dawn.summary: While war and disease dominate today's headlines, a team at the University of Houston quietly rewrote the physics textbooks — shattering a 30-year-old superconductivity record with a material that carries electricity at zero resistance under normal pressure. This is the kind of unglamorous, patient science that eventually powers everything: lossless energy grids, faster MRI machines, maglev transport, and quantum computers that don't require near-absolute-zero cooling. In a world burning fuel it can barely afford, a breakthrough in how we transmit energy without waste is about as hopeful as physics gets.

factors.name: Science
factors.detail: A 30-year superconductivity record broken at normal pressure is a genuine milestone — the kind of result that unlocks entire downstream fields of engineering.

factors.name: Cooperation
factors.detail: Canada's pivot to Sweden for military aircraft signals that democratic nations are actively rebuilding supply-chain partnerships outside destabilized relationships.

factors.name: Human Spirit
factors.detail: Palestinians in Gaza reopening Eid market stalls minutes after strikes — choosing joy as resistance — is a quiet testament to human determination that defies easy categorization.

factors.name: Environment
factors.detail: Climate tech IPO wave noted in MIT Technology Review suggests capital markets are finally pricing clean energy as a growth sector, not just an ideological bet.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: Disney's $100M commitment to reduce anxiety and avoid sedation in children's MRIs is a rare corporate act of genuine humanitarian engineering at scale.

dawn.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, no text, no antialiasing. Silhouette composition, strong backlight, dramatic. A university physics laboratory at golden hour, warm amber and emerald light blazing through tall industrial windows behind the scene, saturating the air with rich gold and deep green. Against that luminous backlight, two figures are rendered as pure dark silhouettes — an elder scientist standing slightly back with arms crossed, and a younger researcher leaning forward over a cryogenic stage, both profiles crisp and still. The foreground glows with the only lit elements visible: a silvery cryogenic dewar exhaling thick white vapor trails that catch the backlight in soft emerald and gold halos, a tiny dark ceramic pellet sample resting in a metal holder on the bench, and a single electronic readout screen casting a flat green line across its display — zero resistance, the flatline of triumph. Thin copper measurement wires spider outward from the sample like delicate threads catching the light. The lab benches and racks recede into warm amber silhouette, the ordinary clutter of science made monumental by the blazing window behind it. Rich gold, vibrant emerald, saturated and alive.
